channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government Approves Comprehensive Reforms for Pakistan Agricultural Research Council
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has formally approved a major restructuring of the Pakistan Agricultural Research Council (PARC) to strengthen the country's agriculture sector and align it wi...

item.title: Sears AI Chatbot Customer Data Was Publicly Accessible Online
item.description: Sears, though largely absent from U.S. retail, continues to operate its appliance repair services with an AI chatbot named Samantha. Security researcher Jeremiah Fowler discovered three publicly acces...

item.title: Analysis of Iran's Drone and Missile Campaign Against UAE in 2026 Conflict
item.description: In the ongoing conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the United States in 2026, Iran has launched a large-scale retaliatory campaign using drones and missiles targeting the United Arab Emirates (UAE) a...

item.title: Police seek help to find two 14-year-olds missing from Feltham for ten days
item.description: Hillingdon Police have issued a public appeal for assistance in locating two 14-year-old teenagers, Ethan and Morgan-Leigh, who have been missing from Feltham, west London, since 8 March 2026. The tee...

item.title: Examining Paraxanthine: A Potential Alternative to Caffeine in Beverages
item.description: Paraxanthine, a naturally occurring compound produced when the body metabolises caffeine, is emerging as a possible alternative stimulant in energy drinks and some coffee products. Proponents suggest ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz commends Kazakhstan President on adoption of new constitution
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif congratulated Kazakhstan President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev on the success of the recent constitutional referendum, calling it a historic step. The referendum, held on Monda...

item.title: FCT High Court fines EFCC N500,000 for delaying Godwin Emefiele’s trial
item.description: For Maitama, Abuja, the Federal Capital Territory High Court don give EFCC gbege as dem don award N500,000 cost against the anti-graft agency. This one happen because EFCC dey delay the trial of forme...

item.title: Khaby Lame Sells Digital Rights While Balancing Faith and Fame
item.description: Khaby Lame, born Khabane Lame in Dakar, Senegal, has become TikTok's most followed creator with over 160 million followers. Known for his silent, expressive reactions to overly complicated 'life hacks...

item.title: Sam Mitchell addresses player behaviour and club culture following US arrest of Hawthorn stars
item.description: Hawthorn coach Sam Mitchell faced intense questioning regarding the culture at his club after the off-season arrest of star players Dylan Moore and Connor Macdonald in the United States. During an app...

item.title: NIT urged to improve innovation and attract more students for global competitiveness
item.description: Dar es Salaam: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Infrastructure has called on the National Institute of Transport (NIT) to enhance its innovation and promotional strategies to attract more stude...

item.title: LAPD Captain Reassigned Following Investigation Into Subordinate Officers’ Offensive Remarks
item.description: A Los Angeles Police Department captain, Robin Petillo, has avoided termination after a complaint was filed regarding racist, sexist, and homophobic remarks made by officers under her supervision in t...

item.title: Rhona Namanya driving innovation and consumer-focused growth at Serengeti Breweries
item.description: Rhona Namanya has been leading beer and ready-to-drink (RTD) beverages at Serengeti Breweries Limited for over three years, focusing on intentional, consumer-centered growth. She uses a method she cal...

item.title: North Sea drilling would have minimal impact on UK household energy bills, study finds
item.description: Amid the recent volatility in global oil and gas prices, particularly due to Middle East conflicts, there have been renewed calls for increased drilling in the North Sea to lower UK energy bills. Howe...

item.title: Siv Ngesi Expands Zuney Burger Franchise to Cape Town
item.description: Following the successful launch of his burger joint Zuney in Johannesburg, Siv Ngesi has now brought his brand to Cape Town, further expanding his business footprint. The Cape Town opening attracted h...

item.title: Vaughan Mayor urges stronger federal action against antisemitic attacks
item.description: On March 7, 2026, Vaughan Mayor Steven Del Duca visited the Beth Avraham Yoseph of Toronto synagogue hours after it was targeted in a shooting, demonstrating his support for the local Jewish community...

item.title: Professor Abdulrahman Akinoso Elected as Oyo PDP Chairman at Party Congress
item.description: A professor from the Department of Food Technology at the University of Ibadan, Prof. Abdulrahman Akinoso, don become the new Oyo State Chairman for the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) faction wey dey ...

item.title: Alan Carr purchases Ayton Castle in Scotland and acquires barony title for Disney+ series
item.description: Comedian and television presenter Alan Carr, aged 49, has confirmed his purchase of Ayton Castle, a 19th-century stately home located in Ayton, Scotland. The acquisition was inspired by his victorious...

item.title: Senator Warren questions Pentagon on xAI’s access to classified systems due to security concerns
item.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) has formally raised concerns with the Pentagon regarding Elon Musk's AI company, xAI, gaining access to classified U.S. military networks. Warren's letter to Defense Se...

item.title: Comelec Clarifies Garcia is Not Promoting Manual Counting for 2028 Elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has clarified recent social media posts falsely claiming that its chairperson, George Garcia, is advocating for manual counting in the 2028 elections to prevent c...

item.title: Lok Sabha Revokes Suspension of Eight Opposition MPs, Speaker Urges Decorum
item.description: On 17th March 2026, the Lok Sabha revoked the suspension of eight opposition MPs who were barred on 3rd February for unruly behaviour. Speaker Om Birla emphasised that going forward, no placards, post...
